RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 118-CB, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Bill Irwin, a Republican, proudly represents Cass County (District 55) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
A lifelong servant-leader, Rep. Irwin brings a wealth of experience in national security, public safety, and community service to his role. Rep Irwin credits becoming an Eagle Scout as the foundation for his service and devotion to duty. A 1974 graduate of Parkway Central High School, he earned his bachelor’s degree from the University of Missouri in 1979 and later completed a master’s degree in National Security Strategy from the United States Navy War College in 2009.
Rep. Irwin served an impressive 33 years in the United States Navy, starting as an Enlisted Hull Maintenance Technician and retiring as a Navy SEAL Captain, where he cultivated leadership skills, and strategic expertise while also displaying an unwavering commitment to duty and service. Additionally, he served in protecting his community as a member of the Lee’s Summit Police Department and SWAT Team member for 17 years, fully demonstrating his dedication to ensuring the safety and security of others.
Rep. Irwin remains actively involved in his community as a member of Abundant Life Church, where he serves on the security team. Rep. Irwin is married to his wife, Barbara, and has a son, Brian, and two Airedale Terriers.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the offense of unlawful tracking of a motor vehicle
Changes the laws regarding the foreign ownership of real property
Provides federal servicemembers civil relief act (SCRA) protections to National Guard members called to state duty

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies guidelines for student participation in athletic contests organized by sex
Modifies provisions relating to certain fees collected by the Missouri Emergency Response Commission
Establishes the "Missouri Religious Freedom Protection Act"
Modifies provisions relating to the rights of unborn children
Modifies guidelines for student participation in athletic contests organized by sex
Establishes the "Safe Place for Newborns Fund" for the installation of newborn safety incubators
Establishes the "Missouri Task Force on Nonprofit Safety and Security"
Prohibits abortions following the detection of a fetal heartbeat and modifies provisions relating to the rights of unborn children
Creates the "Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act"
Allows the chief law enforcement executive in any jurisdiction to request assistance from another jurisdiction, including a jurisdiction outside the state of Missouri
Authorizes a transient guest tax in Harrisonville upon voter approval
Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Clarifies that military personnel are eligible for in-state tuition for undergraduate and graduate degree programs
Modifies provisions relating to public safety
Creates provisions relating to cannabinoids
Authorizes an income tax deduction for capital gains
Modifies provisions relating to the minimum wage, exempting employees under 20 years of age from minimum wage increases
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Modifies provisions relating to the protection of children
Prohibits state departments from spending money on diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives
Designates the "Lloyd Memorial Highway" in Newton County
Establishes the "Motivational Boot Camp Incarceration Program" in the department of corrections and allows certain persons to be sentenced to the program
Modifies provisions relating to alternative therapies and treatments, including psilocybin
Creates provisions relating to foreign ownership of real estate
Provides protections against discrimination and antisemitism in public schools and public postsecondary educational institutions
Provides federal Servicemember Civil Relief Act (SCRA) protections to National Guard members called to state duty
Allows the director of the department of public safety to deny a peace officer license if an applicant is not a U.S. citizen or has had a license permanently revoked or suspended
Modifies provisions relating to the list of diseases required to be reported to the department of health and senior services
Creates provisions relating to court-ordered involuntary outpatient treatment for persons with mental disorders
Modifies provisions relating to an income tax credit for contributions to pregnancy resource center
Allows the general assembly, the house, and the senate to employ capitol security officers
Modifies provisions relating to the age of marriage
Adds mifepristone to the list of Schedule IV controlled substances and establishes the offense of coerced criminal abortion by means of fraud
Modifies and establishes provisions relating to the protection of children and vulnerable persons
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions
Proposes a constitutional amendment regarding the right to life
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to modification of the Constitution
Proposes a constitutional amendment that establishes provisions relating to reproductive health care
